english_name: The Epistle of Paul the Apostle to Titus

verses.latin_vulgate: Paulus servus Dei, Apostolus autem Jesu Christi secundum fidem electorum Dei, et agnitionem veritatis, quæ secundum pietatem est
verses.greek_textus_receptus: παῦλος δοῦλος θεοῦ ἀπόστολος δὲ ἰησοῦ χριστοῦ κατὰ πίστιν ἐκλεκτῶν θεοῦ καὶ ἐπίγνωσιν ἀληθείας τῆς κατ᾽ εὐσέβειαν
verses.king_james: Paul, a servant of God, and an apostle of Jesus Christ, according to the faith of God's elect, and the acknowledging of the truth which is after godliness;

verses.latin_vulgate: in spem vitæ æternæ, quam promisit qui non mentitur, Deus, ante tempora sæcularia:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐπ᾽ ἐλπίδι ζωῆς αἰωνίου ἣν ἐπηγγείλατο ὁ ἀψευδὴς θεὸς πρὸ χρόνων αἰωνίων
verses.king_james: In hope of eternal life, which God, that cannot lie, promised before the world began;

verses.latin_vulgate: manifestavit autem temporibus suis verbum suum in prædicatione, quæ credita est mihi secundum præceptum Salvatoris nostri Dei:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐφανέρωσεν δὲ καιροῖς ἰδίοις τὸν λόγον αὐτοῦ ἐν κηρύγματι ὃ ἐπιστεύθην ἐγὼ κατ᾽ ἐπιταγὴν τοῦ σωτῆρος ἡμῶν θεοῦ
verses.king_james: But hath in due times manifested his word through preaching, which is committed unto me according to the commandment of God our Saviour;

verses.latin_vulgate: Tito dilecto filio secundum communem fidem, gratia, et pax a Deo Patre, et Christo Jesu Salvatore nostro.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τίτῳ γνησίῳ τέκνῳ κατὰ κοινὴν πίστιν χάρις ἔλεος εἰρήνη ἀπὸ θεοῦ πατρὸς καὶ κυρίου ἰησοῦ χριστοῦ τοῦ σωτῆρος ἡμῶν
verses.king_james: To Titus, *mine* own son after the common faith: Grace, mercy, *and* peace, from God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ our Saviour.

verses.latin_vulgate: Hujus rei gratia reliqui te Cretæ, ut ea quæ desunt, corrigas, et constituas per civitates presbyteros, sicut et ego disposui tibi,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τούτου χάριν κατέλιπόν σε ἐν κρήτῃ ἵνα τὰ λείποντα ἐπιδιορθώσῃ καὶ καταστήσῃς κατὰ πόλιν πρεσβυτέρους ὡς ἐγώ σοι διεταξάμην
verses.king_james: For this cause left I thee in Crete, that thou shouldest set in order the things that are wanting, and ordain elders in every city, as I had appointed thee:

verses.latin_vulgate: si quis sine crimine est, unius uxoris vir, filios habens fideles, non in accusatione luxuriæ, aut non subditos.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἴ τίς ἐστιν ἀνέγκλητος μιᾶς γυναικὸς ἀνήρ τέκνα ἔχων πιστά μὴ ἐν κατηγορίᾳ ἀσωτίας ἢ ἀνυπότακτα
verses.king_james: If any be blameless, the husband of one wife, having faithful children not accused of riot or unruly.

verses.latin_vulgate: Oportet enim episcopum sine crimine esse, sicut Dei dispensatorem: non superbum, non iracundum, non vinolentum, non percussorem, non turpis lucri cupidum:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: δεῖ γὰρ τὸν ἐπίσκοπον ἀνέγκλητον εἶναι ὡς θεοῦ οἰκονόμον μὴ αὐθάδη μὴ ὀργίλον μὴ πάροινον μὴ πλήκτην μὴ αἰσχροκερδῆ
verses.king_james: For a bishop must be blameless, as the steward of God; not selfwilled, not soon angry, not given to wine, no striker, not given to filthy lucre;

verses.latin_vulgate: sed hospitalem, benignum, sobrium, justum, sanctum, continentem,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀλλὰ φιλόξενον φιλάγαθον σώφρονα δίκαιον ὅσιον ἐγκρατῆ
verses.king_james: But a lover of hospitality, a lover of good men, sober, just, holy, temperate;

verses.latin_vulgate: amplectentem eum, qui secundum doctrinam est, fidelem sermonem: ut potens sit exhortari in doctrina sana, et eos qui contradicunt, arguere.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀντεχόμενον τοῦ κατὰ τὴν διδαχὴν πιστοῦ λόγου ἵνα δυνατὸς ᾖ καὶ παρακαλεῖν ἐν τῇ διδασκαλίᾳ τῇ ὑγιαινούσῃ καὶ τοὺς ἀντιλέγοντας ἐλέγχειν
verses.king_james: Holding fast the faithful word as he hath been taught, that he may be able by sound doctrine both to exhort and to convince the gainsayers.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sunt enim multi etiam inobedientes, vaniloqui, et seductores: maxime qui de circumcisione sunt:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰσὶν γὰρ πολλοὶ καὶ ἀνυπότακτοι ματαιολόγοι καὶ φρεναπάται μάλιστα οἱ ἐκ περιτομῆς
verses.king_james: For there are many unruly and vain talkers and deceivers, specially they of the circumcision:

verses.latin_vulgate: quos oportet redargui: qui universas domos subvertunt, docentes quæ non oportet, turpis lucri gratia.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὓς δεῖ ἐπιστομίζειν οἵτινες ὅλους οἴκους ἀνατρέπουσιν διδάσκοντες ἃ μὴ δεῖ αἰσχροῦ κέρδους χάριν
verses.king_james: Whose mouths must be stopped, who subvert whole houses, teaching things which they ought not, for filthy lucre's sake.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixit quidam ex illis, proprius ipsorum propheta: Cretenses semper mendaces, malæ bestiæ, ventres pigri.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἶπέν τις ἐξ αὐτῶν ἴδιος αὐτῶν προφήτης κρῆτες ἀεὶ ψεῦσται κακὰ θηρία γαστέρες ἀργαί
verses.king_james: One of themselves, *even* a prophet of their own, said, The Cretians *are* alway liars, evil beasts, slow bellies.

verses.latin_vulgate: Testimonium hoc verum est. Quam ob causam increpa illos dure, ut sani sint in fide,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἡ μαρτυρία αὕτη ἐστὶν ἀληθής δι᾽ ἣν αἰτίαν ἔλεγχε αὐτοὺς ἀποτόμως ἵνα ὑγιαίνωσιν ἐν τῇ πίστει
verses.king_james: This witness is true. Wherefore rebuke them sharply, that they may be sound in the faith;

verses.latin_vulgate: non intendentes judaicis fabulis, et mandatis hominum, aversantium se a veritate.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μὴ προσέχοντες ἰουδαϊκοῖς μύθοις καὶ ἐντολαῖς ἀνθρώπων ἀποστρεφομένων τὴν ἀλήθειαν
verses.king_james: Not giving heed to Jewish fables, and commandments of men, that turn from the truth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Omnia munda mundis: coinquinatis autem et infidelibus, nihil est mundum, sed inquinatæ sunt eorum et mens et conscientia.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πάντα μὲν καθαρὰ τοῖς καθαροῖς τοῖς δὲ μεμιασμένοις καὶ ἀπίστοις οὐδὲν καθαρόν ἀλλὰ μεμίανται αὐτῶν καὶ ὁ νοῦς καὶ ἡ συνείδησις
verses.king_james: Unto the pure all things *are* pure: but unto them that are defiled and unbelieving *is* nothing pure; but even their mind and conscience is defiled.

verses.latin_vulgate: Confitentur se nosse Deum, factis autem negant: cum sint abominati, et incredibiles, et ad omne opus bonum reprobi.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: θεὸν ὁμολογοῦσιν εἰδέναι τοῖς δὲ ἔργοις ἀρνοῦνται βδελυκτοὶ ὄντες καὶ ἀπειθεῖς καὶ πρὸς πᾶν ἔργον ἀγαθὸν ἀδόκιμοι
verses.king_james: They profess that they know God; but in works they deny *him*, being abominable, and disobedient, and unto every good work reprobate.
